name: Banana Chocolate Chip Bread
instructions: 1. Preheat your oven to 325°F (165°C) and position the rack in the center for even baking. Grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan with butter or cooking spray and set aside. 2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt until well combined. Set aside. 3. In a separate bowl, mash the bananas with a fork until smooth. Stir in the milk and cinnamon until well mixed. 4. In a large mixing bowl, beat the sugar and butter together using an electric mixer on medium speed for 3-4 minutes, until the mixture is light, fluffy, and pale in color. Scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed. 5. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition for about 30 seconds until fully incorporated before adding the next egg. 6. Pour the banana mixture into the butter mixture and stir gently to combine. Gradually add the flour mixture, stirring just until the dry ingredients are incorporated - do not overmix or the bread will be tough. Gently fold in the chocolate chips using a spatula. 7. Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan, spreading it evenly. Tap the pan gently on the counter a few times to remove any air bubbles. 8. Bake in the preheated oven for about 70 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean or with just a few moist crumbs. If the top browns too quickly, tent loosely with aluminum foil during the last 20 minutes. 9. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan for 10 minutes. Then carefully run a knife around the edges and remove the bread from the pan. Transfer to a wire cooling rack to cool completely before slicing, about 1 hour.
simpleInstructions: 1. Preheat oven to 325°F and grease a loaf pan. 2. Whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. 3. Mash bananas and mix with milk and cinnamon. 4. Beat sugar and butter until fluffy, then add eggs one at a time. 5. Combine banana mixture with butter mixture. 6. Fold in flour mixture and chocolate chips. 7. Pour into pan and bake 70 minutes until toothpick comes out clean. 8. Cool 10 minutes in pan, then transfer to wire rack to cool completely.
